Add gen_html.py and regenerate index.html with full MVC study guide

Adds a Python generator script (gen_html.py) that builds a complete
single-page study guide covering:
- §10.4 Conic Sections (parabolas, ellipses, hyperbolas — 15 problems)
- §11.1 3D Coordinates & Spheres (8 problems)
- §11.2 Vectors (8 problems)
- §11.3 Dot Product & Projections (6 problems)
- §11.4 Cross Product (8 problems)
- §11.5 Lines in 3-Space (8 problems)
- §11.6 Planes (7 problems)
- §11.7 Quadric Surfaces (7 problems)
- §11.8 Cylindrical & Spherical Coordinates (7 problems)
- §13.1 Functions of Two Variables (9 problems)

Features: sticky nav with section tabs, collapsible key-concepts boxes,
MathJax for LaTeX rendering, Plotly CDN, Google Fonts (DM Serif Display,
DM Sans, Space Mono), full CSS variable theming.

fix(#15): fix title generation — reuse existing session, tighter prompt

LiteRT only supports one session at a time. generateOnce() was calling
engine.createConversation() which threw FAILED_PRECONDITION because the
main chat session was already open.

Fix: reuse the existing conversation field instead of creating a new one.

Also improved title extraction:
- Embed directive in the prompt itself (system prompt has no effect on
  existing conversations)
- Post-process to strip 'How about:' preamble, ** markdown, quotes,
  trailing punctuation and alternative suggestions

Update htdocs/class/xoopseditor/tinymce7/js/tinymce/CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 4-6: Python layer hardening — fallbacks, observability, validation

Eager fallback:
- BridgedCUDAGraph: try/except on capture_begin/end and replay,
  auto-falls back to eager via model_fn if provided
- ShapeBucketPool: per-bucket capture failure tracking, eager fallback
- ConditionalGraph: per-branch failure tracking, eager fallback

VRAM safety:
- ShapeBucketPool checks torch.cuda.mem_get_info before capture
- Configurable cap via GFXGRAPH_VRAM_CAP env (default 80%)
- Lazy bucket instantiation (warmup=False default)

Validation mode:
- gfxgraph.enable(validate=True) or GFXGRAPH=validate
- BridgedCUDAGraph compares graph output vs eager with torch.allclose
- Catches PyTorch #155684 (HIP Graph correctness bugs)

Observability:
- Thread-safe counter tracking (capture, replay, fallback, validation)
- avg_replay_us rolling average
- HGB_LOG_LEVEL=debug|info|warn|error structured logging
- health_check: VRAM reporting, output correctness verification

Input validation:
- Tensor device (CUDA not CPU), contiguity warnings
- model_fn callable check, type validation on all public APIs

Error propagation:
- _C.py: call_native() helper with return code logging
- GFXGRAPH_LIB env override for .so path
- All HIP return codes surfaced to Python layer

Bump to v0.3.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(bench): reset Maglev deopt cooldown after warmup + track deopt categories

After 100-iteration warmup, benchmarks like sieve_primes accumulate
7 deopts with exponential cooldown (next_try_at=133+). Criterion's
short measurement window (10 samples × 1s) never clears the cooldown,
so Maglev tried=0 executed=0 during measurement.

Fix: call ba.reset_maglev_deopt_count() after warmup for all 9

commit.message:
fix(slugs): prevent crawler merge from overwriting translated slugs

Three fixes for slug stability across all 94+ crawlers:

Bug A (root cause): mergeLocaleTextMap without sourceLocale used
"longer wins" heuristic, letting English-derived slugs (with hash
suffixes) overwrite properly translated locale slugs. Changed to
"existing wins" — b only fills gaps, never overwrites a.
hardenJobLocaleFields still propagates fresh base titles to the
source locale, so title updates are not blocked.

Bug B: regenerate-slugs-by-locale.mjs updated slugByLocale.it
without syncing job.slug (master). The stale master caused
cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale to incorrectly remove the old IT
slug from previousSlugsByLocale. Now also updates job.slug when
the IT slug changes.

Data fix: Recovered TSMG job master slug — synced to translated
IT slug and captured old English-derived slug in previousSlugs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: summary agent now includes all agent types per symbol

get_recent_activities_by_symbol() was fetching TOP N activities per
symbol without grouping by agent_type. When a symbol had both put
and call watching, the most recent N activities could all be from
one type, leaving the other completely absent from summaries.

Changed to query each agent type (covered_call, cash_secured_put,
open_call_monitor, open_put_monitor) separately per symbol, then
merge and sort by timestamp. This ensures all active agent types
are represented in summary data.

commit.message:
Fix similar TooManySponsoring issue in revoke sponsorship

RevokeSponsorship's transfer and establish paths used ? on
update_num_sponsoring/apply_entry_sponsorship_with_sponsor, which
propagated TxError::Internal (becoming TxInternalError) instead of
returning OpTooManySponsoring when num_sponsoring was at u32::MAX.

Add pre-checks matching stellar-core's canTransferSponsorshipHelper and
canEstablishSponsorshipHelper pattern: verify num_sponsoring capacity
before mutating state. Affects all four sponsorship paths (ledger entry
transfer, ledger entry establish, signer transfer, signer establish).
